evidence: modules/asset/material/src/Hook/EntityHooks.php copies a material asset's material_type onto a referencing material-bundle quantity via inventory_asset — a cross-entity denormalizing fold the spine vocab scan missed (it lives in a quantity presave hook, not the AssetType plugin). Needs the quantity + inventory_asset model, absent from the asset spine.

topic: 'equipment used' base field on the LOG entity (BIG-PUNT VALVE)
evidence: modules/asset/equipment/src/Hook/FieldHooks.php adds an entity_reference base field 'equipment' to the LOG entity (+ CSV import, Views, field-group wiring). Cross-family (log, not asset); the spine vocab scan ('label-only, no new fields') missed it because it is a hook, not the AssetType plugin.

Every typed entity_reference field in these bundles (equipment_type, material_type, plant_type, season, product_type) carries auto_create:TRUE — Drupal creates the referenced taxonomy term on the fly if the label does not resolve. The spine stores the supplied term reference verbatim and does NOT model term creation (no taxonomy-term entity in this spine). Inert for the asset-bundle surface; a term-vocabulary spine would own it.

topic: archive lifecycle is monotonic in this spine
evidence: assetActive is the 'no asset_archived event' fold — once archived, inactive, matching the wave-1 asset-spine reading. farmOS's underlying asset status field can flip back to active (un-archive); that reversal is NOT modeled here. Continuity with the established spine, not a fresh decision. A fixture asserting un-archive would reverse it (add a latest-wins status kind).
